groups.explanation: The harness correctly calls H5Fopen with valid arguments; the OOM occurs entirely within the library before any image API is reached. In H5Fsuper.c:606, the EOA is set from sblock->driver_addr, which is read directly from the crafted fuzz input, making EOA itself attacker-controlled (~4 GB). H5C__verify_len_eoa (H5Centry.c:1147) then approves actual_len against this inflated EOA, so the realloc at line 1155 attempts a ~4 GB allocation. A tiny malicious HDF5 file can therefore cause arbitrary memory allocation via the superblock's driver_addr field.
groups.suggestedFixDirection: In H5Fsuper.c (and/or H5C__verify_len_eoa), bound the EOA and all derived allocation sizes against the actual physical file size (H5FD_get_eof) so that file-embedded length fields in the superblock/driver-info block cannot force allocations larger than the real file.
